rulings.Case Title: EDNA CANNON et al VS. CRYSTAL IDELLA CANNON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 5, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Granted as to the first through fourth causes of action as to Bank of America only and denied as to the rest of the motion. The court previously sustained without leave to amend Bank of America's demurrer to the first through fourth causes of action and Plaintiff has impermissibly reasserted these causes of action against Bank of America in the Second Amended Complaint. The action is not barred by Code of Civil Procedure section 583.310 because the action was stayed pending Plaintiff's substitution as successor in interest. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: DARYL BERKSON BRAND VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration And To Compel Further Production Of Documents; Declaration ; Memo Of P And A; Seperate Statement;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, September 5, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS DARYL BRAND, THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM BRAND, DECEASED'S Motion For Reconsideration And To Compel Further Production Of Documents. (Part 1 of 2.) Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. Plaintiffs have not met the standard for reconsideration set forth in Cal. Code Civil Proc. Section 1008(b), and the request is untimely under Section 1008(a). The motion to compel and for sanctions is denied because Plaintiffs did not make a reasonable and good faith attempt to meet and confer in advance of bringing the instant motion. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALFREDO H. BUSTAMANTE VS. TIAN YU LU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Lift Stay On Disco
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, September 5, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ALFREDO BUSTAMANTE'S Motion To Lift Stay On Discovery. (Part 1 of 2.) Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. The December 20, 2010 Stay Order directed that discovery in this matter "shall be stayed pending final resolution of the criminal proceedings against the defendant that are presently underway." While the criminal trial against the defendant has completed, the appeal of the defendant's resulting conviction is still pending and thus has not been finally resolved. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 5, 2012, Line 15, PLAINTIFF STEPHANIE WILLIS MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Granted. There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment and there is no prejudice to defendant by because the trial date is still months away and discovery remains open. rulings.Case Title: ERNEST CHIN VS. MEDSAM ENTERPRISES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Leave To File Amendments To Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 5, 2012, Line 16, PLAINTIFF ERNEST CHIN Mtn For Leave To File Amendments To Amended Complt Denied as to the amendments to the second cause of action and 59 and 60 in the third cause of action. The second cause of action fails as a matter of law even if the amendments were allowed. 59 and 60 add nothing to the third cause of action, especially in light of Plaintiff's concession that it is not asserting a claim for Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress. Granted as to all other amendments. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ERNEST CHIN VS. MEDSAM ENTERPRISES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 5, 2012, Line 16, DEFENDANT JEWISH FAMILY AND CHILDEREN'S SERVICES DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action as to all Defendants. Even considering the improper amendments to the second cause of action alleged in the First Amended Complaint, Plaintiff has not and cannot state a cause of action for violation of the UCL on these facts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: ERNEST CHIN VS. MEDSAM ENTERPRISES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 5, 2012, Line 16, DEFENDANT MEDSAM ENTERPRISES, INC., PERELMAN, EVGUENI ERRONEOUSLY SUED AND, MEDSAM TRANSPORTATION DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action as to all Defendants. Plaintiff concedes that he did not intend to assert this cause of action against Medsam Transportation. Further, Plaintiff has not and cannot state a cause of action for violation of the UCL on these facts. The demurrer to the third cause of action is off calendar as moot in light of the ruling on Defendants' Motion to Strike and because Plaintiff concedes that it did not intend to include a cause of action for Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress in its First Amended Complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. [SM]

rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, September 5, 2012, Line 6, PLAINTIFFS MXB HOLDINGS, LP, MICHAEL JACKSON, HECKLER CONSULTANCY, SV ANGEL, LLC'S Motion To Compel The Production Of Documents By Defendant Criterion. rulings.Case Title: MXB HOLDINGS, LP A DELAWARE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al VS. ADAM LEVIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel The Production Of Docs By Deft Criterion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, September 5, 2012, Line 6, PLAINTIFFS MXB HOLDINGS, LP, MICHAEL JACKSON, HECKLER CONSULTANCY, SV ANGEL, LLC'S Motion To Compel The Production Of Documents By Defendant Criterion. (Part 1 of 2.) Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. Regardless of e-service issue, plaintiffs have provided proof that the requests for production were served by hand. Defendants have not provided any substantive response to the discovery. Sanctions are awarded to plaintiff in the amount of $4,035 (assuming no hearing). =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOE DRUMMOND et al VS. FRANCESCA ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 5, 2012, Line 21, DEFENDANT MARY COLLIER, JOHN GRANT DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the entire complaint and the third cause of action, and overruled as to the fourth through sixth causes of action. Plaintiffs seek injunctive relief and must file a certificate per Civil Code section 1369.560. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to comply with Civil Code section 1369.560 and to allege personal liability of the Ms. Collier and Mr. Grant per Civil Code section 1365.7. Plaintiffs have adequately pled both intentional and negligent infliction of emotional distress. Conspiracy is a sufficient basis for the imposition of civil liability. The claims are not uncertain. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.
